name: Castillo de Castrovido — circular from Salas de los Infantes
name_original: Castillo de Castrovido — circular desde Salas de los Infantes
region_intro: Jogging around Salas de los Infantes offers diverse running routes through the Sierra de la Demanda in Burgos, Spain. The region features mountainous landscapes with varied elevations, providing options from gentle slopes to more demanding climbs. Runners can explore extensive forests, such as those near Monasterio de la Sierra, and enjoy paths along the Arlanza River Valley. The area's network of trails includes converted railway lines, offering smooth surfaces ideal for running. Best jogging routes around Salas de los Infantes The most popular jogging route is Castillo de Castrovido — circular from Salas de los Infantes, a 7.3 miles (11.7 km) trail that takes 1 hour 26 minutes to complete, offering views of the historic castle. Another top favourite among local runners is Ascent to Alto de Gayubar — circular from Vallejimeno, a difficult 10.0 miles (16.1 km) path. This route involves significant elevation gain through mountainous terrain. Local runners also love the Salas de los Infantes loop from Salas de los Infantes, a 12.9 miles (20.7 km) trail leading through varied landscapes around the town, often completed in about 2 hours 32 minutes. Jogging around Salas de los Infantes is defined by mountainous terrain of the Sierra de la Demanda, the Arlanza River Valley, and extensive forests. The network offers options for different ability levels, from moderate runs to more challenging ascents. The guide includes highly-rated routes that showcase the best of the region's landscape. More than 40 runners have used komoot to explore Salas de los Infantes's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many running routes are available around Salas de los Infantes?
A: The Salas de los Infantes area offers a diverse network of over 10 running routes. These trails cater to various fitness levels, from moderate paths through the Arlanza River Valley to more challenging ascents in the Sierra de la Demanda.

Q: Are there any easy or beginner-friendly jogging routes in Salas de los Infantes?
A: While many routes in the Sierra de la Demanda involve varied elevations, you can find more gentle options. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31851006" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Hacinas Village loop from Hacinas&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate 3.6-mile (5.8 km) route with minimal elevation gain, making it suitable for a relaxed run. The region's converted railway lines, like sections of the Greenway of the Sierra de la Demanda, also provide smooth, flatter surfaces ideal for beginners.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on the running trails near Salas de los Infantes?
A: Jogging routes in Salas de los Infantes offer a mix of terrains. You'll find mountainous landscapes with varied elevations in the Sierra de la Demanda, extensive forests offering shaded paths, and flatter riverside trails along the Arlanza River Valley. Some routes also utilize converted railway lines, providing well-maintained surfaces.

Q: Are there any circular running routes around Salas de los Infantes?
A: Yes, many of the running routes in the area are circular, allowing you to start and finish in the same location. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/24550721" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Salas de los Infantes – Castrovido loop from Salas de los Infantes&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate 7.3-mile (11.7 km) circular trail that takes you through varied landscapes.

Q: What can I see along the jogging routes in Salas de los Infantes?
A: The running routes offer a blend of natural beauty and historical sights. You might pass by prominent rock formations like Peña del Carazo, explore areas with ancient oak forests near Monasterio de la Sierra, or discover fossilized trees and wetlands near Hacinas. Some trails also offer views of historical sites like the castle in Castrovido or the unique &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2891679" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sad Hill Cemetery&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any viewpoints or scenic spots on the running trails?
A: Absolutely. The mountainous terrain of the Sierra de la Demanda provides numerous opportunities for rewarding views. Routes leading to higher elevations, such as those near &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5553269" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Alto de Gayubar&lt;/a&gt; or &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5038174" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pico Las Torres&lt;/a&gt;, offer panoramic vistas. You can also find specific viewpoints like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5301290" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Villanueva de Carazo Plateau&lt;/a&gt;, which provides expansive scenery.

Q: What is the best time of year for running in Salas de los Infantes?
A: The region's diverse landscapes make it suitable for running throughout much of the year. Spring and autumn generally offer pleasant temperatures and vibrant scenery. Summer can be warm, but the extensive forests provide welcome shade. Winter runs are also possible, though higher elevations in the Sierra de la Demanda might experience colder conditions.

Q: Are the running trails in Salas de los Infantes suitable for families?
A: Yes, some routes are suitable for families, especially those looking for less strenuous options. Paths along the Arlanza River Valley or sections of the 'Greenway of the Sierra de la Demanda' (a converted railway line) offer relatively flat and safe surfaces. These areas are great for enjoying nature with children.

Q: Can I bring my dog on the running routes?
A: Generally, the natural trails around Salas de los Infantes are dog-friendly, but it's always advisable to keep your dog on a leash, especially in areas with livestock or protected wildlife. Ensure you carry water for your pet and clean up after them to maintain the pristine environment.

Q: What do other runners enjoy most about jogging in Salas de los Infantes?
A: Runners frequently praise the region for its stunning natural beauty and varied landscapes. The mix of challenging mountain trails, peaceful forest paths, and scenic riverside routes provides a rich experience. Many appreciate the opportunity to combine their run with views of historical sites and unique geological features.

Q: Are there any long-distance running options in the area?
A: Yes, for those looking for longer runs, the network of trails in the Sierra de la Demanda offers several options. You can combine different sections or explore routes that extend over 10 miles (16 km), providing ample distance for endurance training or longer scenic jogs through varied terrain.

Q: Is there parking available near the trailheads?
A: In Salas de los Infantes and surrounding villages like Hacinas or Vallejimeno, you can typically find parking near the start points of many popular running routes. As the area is geared towards outdoor activities, local infrastructure often accommodates visitors with vehicles, though specific parking availability may vary by trailhead.
tips: Castrovido castle is one of the emblems of Salas de los Infantes. Its Celtiberian and Roman origin is possible. It is located at the top of a small hill and has been restored inside.

Located in the valley of the Arlanza river, in the southeast of the province of Burgos. The territory where Salas is today was inhabited by the Celtiberian tribe of the Pelendones, who occupied a fort on a hill, until the arrival of the Romans, who established a settlement on the river bank.
